topic: humanoid robot market: funding, manufacturers, deployments, IPOs, adoption

items.title: AgiBot debuts four robots at WAIC, led by the A3 Ultra humanoid
items.summary: At Shanghai's World AI Conference the company unveiled the 1.74 m, 51-DoF A3 Ultra — running Nvidia's Thor chip with up to eight hours of runtime, according to AgiBot — alongside the X2 EDU education platform, the industrial G2 Max and the 20-DoF OmniHand 3 Ultra-M dexterous hand.

items.title: Weave Robotics puts the home humanoid on sale at $7,999
items.summary: The San Francisco startup has opened orders for Isaac 1, a wheeled home robot that folds laundry, makes beds and tidies rooms autonomously, according to Weave — $7,999 up front or $449 a month, with first deliveries this fall.

items.title: Hyundai moves to take full ownership of Boston Dynamics
items.summary: SoftBank is exercising its put option and Hyundai Motor Group shareholders will buy out its remaining stake in the Atlas maker, according to Hyundai — which says it plans to put Atlas humanoids to work at its Metaplant America factory from 2028.

items.title: LimX Dynamics closes a $200M Pre-IPO round at a RMB 15B valuation
items.summary: The Shenzhen humanoid maker has now raised $400 million in six months — days before showing its Oli robot clearing a room of household chores fully autonomously.

items.title: Toyota spinout Walden Robotics launches with $300M at a $1.1B valuation
items.summary: The Toyota Research Institute spinout led by MIT's Russ Tedrake emerged from stealth with backing from Toyota, Nvidia, Boeing and Samsung — and says its wheeled general-purpose robots have been working at a Toyota plant in North America since February.

items.title: Agility Robotics heads for the stock market at a ~$2.5B valuation
items.summary: The Oregon-based maker of Digit plans to list via a SPAC merger with Churchill Capital Corp XI, raising over $620 million — while CEO Peggy Johnson says home humanoids are still a decade out.

items.title: Amazon and Nvidia among backers of Neura Robotics, CNBC reports
items.summary: More Big Tech capital flows into the humanoid race as the German robot maker draws high-profile investors.
